task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the pilot roster CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, spreadsheet viewer, or terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/pilot_roster.csv', showing columns for flight plan IDs and pilot emails.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the Flight Plans section in the Aerobridge/Django web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser is open to the Aerobridge or Django admin interface, specifically showing the list of Flight Plans (e.g., 'Survey Mission Block').

task_completion.description: Verify the agent looked up the correct pilot or person records associated with the emails from the roster.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser shows the agent searching or viewing the 'Pilots' or 'Persons' list/detail pages to match the emails (e.g., amit.s@droneops.io) to their respective system records.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent assigned a pilot to a specific Flight Plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edit screen for a Flight Plan showing a pilot being selected from a dropdown or search field, or a success message indicating a single Flight Plan was updated with a pilot.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent completed the assignments for multiple Flight Plans as dictated by the roster.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flight Plans list view showing multiple flight plans (e.g., Survey Mission Block 1-5) with assigned pilots, or multiple 'successfully updated' notification banners in the web interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not access the hidden ground truth evaluation file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal, file explorer, or text editor should show the path or contents of '/var/lib/aerobridge/roster_ground_truth.json'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web interface to make assignments rather than bypassing the application logic.
integrity.visual_evidence: Assignments should be made through the web browser UI (Django admin or Aerobridge frontend) rather than direct SQLite database manipulation in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the form fields to assign pilots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Dropdowns, search fields, or selection widgets in the web interface should be visibly interacted with, rather than just inspecting/editing the HTML DOM to fake the visual output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The roster file is located at /home/ga/Documents/pilot_roster.csv. The agent must assign 5 pilots to Flight Plans based on this roster. The expected pilot emails are: amit.s@droneops.io, sarah.j@droneops.io, david.c@droneops.io, priya.p@droneops.io, and mike.r@droneops.io. These should correspond to the first 5 Flight Plans (e.g., Survey Mission Block 1-5). The agent must NOT access /var/lib/aerobridge/roster_ground_truth.json.
